---
title: "Growth & Long-Run Dynamics — Track Map"
---

What makes economies rich over the long run. The chapters build on each other
as shown below; click a node to jump there.

```{mermaid}
graph LR
  FG[Levels vs Growth Rates] --> CD[Cobb-Douglas]
  CD --> SI[Solow Model]
  SI --> ST[Solow Transition]
  SI --> SP[Solow + Pop/Tech]
  SI --> HC[Human Capital]
  ST --> CV[Convergence]
  SI --> SX[Solow Exercises]
  CD --> RM[Romer Model]
  RM --> SE[Scale Effects]
  RM --> HY[Solow-Romer Hybrid]
  SI --> HY
  CD --> GA[Growth Accounting]
  GA --> SG[Slowing Growth]
  CD --> RB[Robots vs Labor]
  click FG "../content/foundations_growth.html"
  click CD "../content/cobb_d_prod.html"
  click SI "../content/solow_intro.html"
  click ST "../content/solow_transition.html"
  click SP "../content/solow_pop&_tech_growth.html"
  click HC "../content/humancapital.html"
  click CV "../content/convergence_hypo.html"
  click SX "../content/solow_model.html"
  click RM "../content/romer_model.html"
  click SE "../content/scale_effects.html"
  click HY "../content/hybrid_model.html"
  click GA "../content/growth_acc.html"
  click SG "../content/slowinggrowth.html"
  click RB "../content/robots.html"
```

## Suggested path

1. [Levels vs. Growth Rates](../content/foundations_growth.qmd) — why how rich a country is and how fast it grows are distinct.
2. [Cobb–Douglas Production](../content/cobb_d_prod.qmd) — output from capital and labor, diminishing returns, and factor shares.
3. [The Solow Growth Model](../content/solow_intro.qmd) — saving, depreciation, and the steady state.
4. [Solow Transition Dynamics](../content/solow_transition.qmd) — the path an economy travels toward its steady state.
5. [Population & Technology Growth](../content/solow_pop&_tech_growth.qmd) — Solow with a growing workforce and advancing technology.
6. [Human Capital](../content/humancapital.qmd) — a second accumulable factor in the Solow framework.
7. [The Convergence Hypothesis](../content/convergence_hypo.qmd) — when poorer economies do, and do not, catch up.
8. [The Romer Model](../content/romer_model.qmd) — ideas, nonrivalry, and growth driven from within.
9. [Scale Effects](../content/scale_effects.qmd) — how population size influences the rate of innovation.
10. [The Solow–Romer Hybrid](../content/hybrid_model.qmd) — capital accumulation combined with endogenous ideas.
11. [Growth Accounting](../content/growth_acc.qmd) — decomposing growth into capital, labor, and TFP.
12. [Slowing Growth and Declining TFP](../content/slowinggrowth.qmd) — what a falling rate of technological advance implies.
13. [Robots vs. Labor](../content/robots.qmd) — automation and substitution in a CES production model.
14. [Solow Exercises & Case Studies](../content/solow_model.qmd) — applied capstone problems for the block.
